dataset.name: EID Quick Reference - Active Directory Domain Services

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.notice: Event descriptions are paraphrased summaries written for this dataset. Source links point to authoritative references.

entries.title: LDAP search statistics — expensive query threshold exceeded
entries.summary: A domain controller recorded statistics for an LDAP search that exceeded configured thresholds.
entries.details: Generated on domain controllers when an LDAP search exceeds configured thresholds for visited entries, returned entries, or elapsed time. Key fields: IPAddress (client performing the search), AccountName, SearchFilter, StartingNode (the base DN), and statistics for VisitedEntries, ReturnedEntries, and TimeElapsed. This event is the primary detection mechanism for LDAP-based Active Directory enumeration by tools such as BloodHound, SharpHound, ADFind, and ldapsearch. Adversaries conducting reconnaissance (T1018, T1069) perform broad LDAP queries to enumerate users, groups, computers, GPOs, trusts, and ACLs. A single IP issuing many EID 1644 events with large VisitedEntries counts within a short window is a high-confidence indicator of AD recon. Requires enabling via registry: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Diagnostics\15 Field Engineering = 5. DC-only event.

prerequisites.description: Enable LDAP expensive query logging by setting HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Diagnostics\15 Field Engineering = 5. Optionally configure search time and result thresholds in HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters.

investigationPivots: IPAddress generating many EID 1644 events in rapid succession is the primary BloodHound/SharpHound indicator
SearchFilter containing '(objectClass=*)' or '(objectCategory=*)' on the root DC or domain DN indicates a full AD dump query
AccountName reveals the account performing recon — service accounts performing broad LDAP queries are anomalous
Correlate IPAddress with the machine's normal role — workstations performing DC LDAP queries are more suspicious than servers
commonFalsePositives: Directory synchronization tools (Azure AD Connect, LDAP sync agents) that perform large, periodic LDAP queries
IT management software that enumerates AD for asset discovery
Threshold set too low — adjust NTDS diagnostics threshold to reduce false positive volume

entries.title: LDAP server signing requirements not enforced
entries.summary: The LDAP server is not configured to require signing or sealing on all connections.
entries.details: Generated when the Active Directory LDAP server detects that signing or sealing is not required for all incoming LDAP connections. This is a configuration event fired at DC startup or when the LDAP signing policy is checked. LDAP connections without signing are vulnerable to relay attacks — an attacker who captures LDAP authentication can relay it to the DC to perform operations as the victim. This is a posture event rather than an attack event: it indicates the DC is susceptible to LDAP relay. The remediation is to set the 'Domain controller: LDAP server signing requirements' Group Policy to 'Require signing'. DC-only event.

investigationPivots: Confirm current LDAP signing policy via Group Policy and registry (HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters\LDAPServerIntegrity)
Presence of this event indicates unsigned LDAP connections are permitted — cross-reference with EID 2887 summary to assess volume
Remediate by setting the DC LDAP signing requirement to 'Require signing' after validating client compatibility
commonFalsePositives: This event fires on DCs where LDAP signing is intentionally not enforced due to legacy application requirements
Environments with older LDAP clients that do not support signing

entries.title: LDAP unsigned bind summary
entries.summary: Hourly summary of LDAP connections that were made without signing or sealing.
entries.details: Generated approximately once per hour on domain controllers to report a summary count of LDAP binds received that did not use signing or sealing (LDAP Simple Binds or unsigned SASL binds). Key fields: NumberOfUnsignedBinds (total unsigned binds in the period). This event is a periodic summary companion to EID 2889, which fires per individual occurrence. When the count is non-zero, clients are authenticating to the DC without LDAP signing — these connections are vulnerable to relay attacks and credential interception. A sustained high count may indicate a legacy application or an adversary actively exploiting unsigned LDAP to relay credentials. Use EID 2889 to identify the specific clients. DC-only event.

investigationPivots: A non-zero count should trigger review of EID 2889 for individual client identification
Track the count over time — a sudden spike may indicate a new application or active attack rather than a known legacy client
Use EID 2889 client IP addresses to identify and remediate unsigned LDAP sources
commonFalsePositives: Legacy applications that perform LDAP Simple Binds and have not been updated to support signing
Third-party directory integration tools that use unsigned LDAP for compatibility

entries.title: Client performed an unsigned LDAP bind
entries.summary: A client made an LDAP Simple Bind or unsigned SASL bind to this domain controller.
entries.details: Generated per occurrence when a client performs an LDAP bind without signing or sealing — either a Simple Bind (credentials sent in plaintext or over an unprotected channel) or an unsigned SASL bind. Key fields: IPAddress (the client making the unsigned bind) and AccountName (the account used). LDAP Simple Binds transmit credentials without encryption or integrity protection, making them susceptible to interception. Adversaries performing LDAP relay attacks will appear here if they are forwarding captured LDAP authentication to the DC. A new, unknown IPAddress making Simple Binds with privileged AccountName values is a high-confidence relay indicator. Requires that the NTDS 'LDAP Interface Events' diagnostic key be set to 2 or higher. DC-only event.

prerequisites.description: Enable LDAP Interface Events logging by setting HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Diagnostics\16 LDAP Interface Events = 2 (or higher).

investigationPivots: IPAddress is the primary pivot — identify the machine and determine if it is a known legacy application host
AccountName for a privileged account (Domain Admin, service account with broad access) making a Simple Bind is highest priority
A new IPAddress making Simple Binds that was not present in prior EID 2887 summaries may indicate an attacker-controlled relay host
commonFalsePositives: Known legacy applications that perform Simple Binds as a long-standing configuration
Third-party directory sync or authentication proxy tools using Simple Binds for compatibility

entries.title: Active Directory replication request denied
entries.summary: A replication request to this domain controller was denied.
entries.details: Generated when a domain controller denies an inbound replication request. Key fields: SourceDC (the machine requesting replication) and the reason for denial. In DFIR contexts this event is relevant as a failed DCSync indicator: DCSync (T1003.006) works by mimicking domain controller replication using DRSUAPI calls (GetNCChanges). A non-DC machine requesting replication will be denied and may generate this event. However, note that a successful DCSync attack will NOT generate this event — it will be visible in Security log EID 4662 (Directory Service Object Access with the required replication permissions). This event primarily captures failed or unauthorized replication attempts. SourceDC that is not a known DC is a critical indicator. DC-only event.

investigationPivots: SourceDC that is not a known domain controller in the environment is a critical DCSync attempt indicator
Correlate with Security log EID 4662 on the DC for successful replication permission use — absence of denial + presence of 4662 with replication permissions indicates a successful DCSync
Investigate the source machine immediately if it is not a registered DC
commonFalsePositives: Decommissioned DCs that were not cleanly removed from AD attempting to replicate
Azure AD Connect or third-party directory tools that use replication APIs for read-only sync
